results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE PURPOSE OF THIS PROJECT IS TO DEVELOP A TRANSDISCIPLINARY APPROACH TO EVALUATE AND MITIGATE THE CUMULATIVE HEALTH CONSEQUENCES OF CHEMICAL AND PSYCHOSOCIAL STRESSORS TO CHILDREN'S HEALTH THROUGH A COMMUNITY DRIVEN PARTICIPATORY FRAMEWORK. ACTIVITIES:THE OBJECTIVE OF THIS RESEARCH IS TO ASSES THE IMPACTS OF EARLY LIFE STAGE HEALTH STRESSORS AND CREATE NEW MODELS AND LANGUAGE TO ENCOMPASS THE DATA DRIVEN INSIGHTS. THIS PROJECT WILL 1) DOCUMENT AND EVALUATE THE CUMULATIVE IMPACT OF CHILDREN'S LOW-DOSE CHRONIC PESTICIDE EXPOSURE; 2) COMPARE EARLY-LIFE MICROBIOME WITH ENVIRONMENTAL STRESSOR EXPOSURES; AND 3) DEVELOP A STATEWIDE, CUMULATIVE IMPACT ASSESSMENT TO MITIGATE CHEMICAL AND NON CHEMICAL STRESSOR EXPOSURE.SUBRECIPIENT:THE PROJECT WILL PROVIDE THE FARMWORKER ASSOCIATION OF FLORIDA (FWAW) WITH A SUBAWARD ($116,649) TO SERVE THE TRANSLATION CORE WITH COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT AND PUBLIC HEALTH OUTREACH TO THE FARMWORKER POPULATION IN IMMOKALEE, FLORIDA.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES WILL INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL REPORTS DETAILING THE CORRELATIONS BETWEEN ENVIRONMENTAL STRESSORS AND CHILDHOOD DEVELOPMENT, EVALUATIONS OF MICROBIOME EFFECTS, AND A PUBLIC HEALTH CUMULATIVE IMPACT ASSESSMENT. INTENDED BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROJECT INCLUDE AGRICULTURAL COMMUNITIES, FARM WORKERS, AND STATE AND LOCAL PUBLIC HEALTH OFFICIALS
